question: Write a program that prompts the user to enter their first name, last name, and age. Then, print a greeting message displaying all the entered information in a formatted way.

test_cases.expected:
Kindly input your initial name: Kindly input your concluding name: Kindly input your numeric age: 
Greetings, Charlie Brown! It has been observed that you are 10 years of age.

Kindly input your initial name: Kindly input your concluding name: Kindly input your numeric age: 
Greetings, Diana Prince! It has been observed that you are 25 years of age.

errors: Uses C-style character arrays (`char[]`) instead of `std::string`, which is less safe and idiomatic C++.
Employs C-style I/O functions (`printf`, `scanf`) instead of C++ streams (`std::cout`, `std::cin`), reducing readability and type safety.
Variable names are too short and not descriptive (e.g., `a` for age).
Using fixed-size buffers for names can lead to buffer overflows if input exceeds the allocated size, which is an inefficiency in memory management and a potential security risk.
